title: Trisetum aeneum
intro: Trisetum aeneum, also known as golden oat grass, is a perennial grass species native to the mountainous regions of Europe and Asia. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which may help in managing conditions such as arthritis and other inflammatory diseases. The bioactive constituents of Trisetum aeneum include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and sesquiterpenes, which are believed to be responsible for its medicinal properties. Trisetum aeneum is often used to make herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are commonly prepared and consumed for their potential health benefits.
taxonomy: Trisetum aeneum is classified as a species of grass in the family Poaceae. It belongs to the tribe Poeae, within the subfamily Pooideae. The genus Trisetum is a group of grasses commonly known as hair-grasses or oat-grasses, typically found in temperate regions. Trisetum aeneum is native to New Zealand and is known for its striking golden color during flowering. It is often found growing in moist, rocky areas and along streams.
